data.title: Grayscale files for the first US spot ETF for privacy coin ZCash
data.description:
Grayscale has filed a form with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to convert its existing ZCash (ZEC) Trust into a spot ETF. The article Grayscale files for the first US spot ETF for privacy coin ZCash was first published on Crypto Valley Journal.
source: https://cryptovalleyjournal.com/investing/financial-products/grayscale-files-first-us-spot-etf-for-privacy-coin-zcash/?utm_campaign=rss_reader&utm_medium=rss&utm_source=feed_source&utm_content=Grayscale+files+for+the+first+US+spot+ETF+for+privacy+coin+ZCash

data.title: Democrats accuse Trump of profiting from cryptocurrency, earning $800 million in the first half of the year.
data.description: Democrats on the House Judiciary Committee released a highly partisan report accusing President Trump and his family of using presidential power to massively profit from the cryptocurrency industry. The report claims that the Trump family profited as much as $800 million in the first half of 2025 through token issuance, investment partnerships, and political donations, and that their total holdings of cryptocurrencies and stocks have risen to $11 billion, doubling their wealth in less than a year. The report alleges that foreign powers have used the purchase of Trump family-related tokens or investment in projects to obtain policy favors, including regulatory easing and preferential treatment. Examples cited by Democrats include Trump's pardons of BitMEX founder Arthur Hayes (March 2025) and Binance founder CZ (October 2025), the termination of investigations into leading companies such as Coinbase, Gemini, Robinhood, Ripple, Crypto.com, Uniswap, Yuga Labs, and Kraken, the disbanding of the Justice Department's "National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Task Force," and the repeal of Biden's executive order "Ensuring the Responsible Development of Digital Assets," among other regulatory measures. The Democratic report concluded that Trump turned the Oval Office into "the world's most corrupt crypto startup," and that his so-called "pro-crypto policy" was essentially a family money-making scheme, operating on a "pay-to-play" model and the influence of foreign interests. (Decrypt)

data.title: IMF Warns of Increased Flash Crash Risks in Tokenized Markets
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) has issued a warning about the heightened risk of flash crashes in tokenized markets, prompting potential regulatory intervention by governments. The IMF highlighted that while tokenization can facilitate faster and more cost-effective financial transactions, it also introduces new systemic risks. Automated trading could lead to increased market volatility and a higher likelihood of flash crashes. Complex smart contract chains may trigger a domino effect under market stress, turning localized issues into systemic shocks.

The IMF anticipates that, based on historical trends, governments will not remain passive during this significant monetary evolution and are expected to take a more active role in the tokenization sector in the future.

data.title: Switzerland has postponed the implementation of the global automatic exchange of encrypted tax information until 2027.
data.description: The Swiss Federal Council and the Secretariat for International Financial Affairs announced that the global Crypto Asset Tax Information Sharing Mechanism (CARF), originally scheduled for launch in 2026, will be postponed to 2027. While the relevant laws will still take effect as planned on January 1, 2026, Switzerland has not yet identified its first batch of partner countries for data exchange, which is considered a major reason for the delay. CARF was launched by the OECD in 2022 to promote the automatic exchange of crypto account information among countries to curb cross-border crypto asset tax evasion. Currently, 75 countries, including Switzerland, have committed to implementing CARF within the next two to four years, while Argentina, El Salvador, Vietnam, and India are among the countries that have not yet joined the framework.

data.title: Tom Lee $100K Bitcoin Prediction Wakes Sleeping Whales for Bitcoin Hyper
data.description:
What to Know: Tom Lee’s $100K Bitcoin target reinforces the idea that this cycle still has upside, pushing traders toward higher-beta plays beyond BTC itself. Bitcoin’s core limitations (slow throughput, variable fees, and no native smart contract) are driving intense interest in Layer 2 designs that unlock scalable, programmable $BTC liquidity. Competing Bitcoin L2 approaches now range from EVM sidechains to rollups and SVM-based execution layers, each trying to capture the next wave of $BTC-driven on-chain activity. Bitcoin Hyper introduces an SVM-powered Bitcoin Layer 2 with extremely low-latency execution and $BTC-settled smart contracts, targeting DeFi, payments, and gaming use cases
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/tom-lee-100k-bitcoin-target-puts-bitcoin-hyper-on-watchlists/

data.title: IMF Explores Tokenized Markets and Their Implications
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) released a video on its X handle discussing the emerging trend of tokenized markets. The video highlights the potential benefits of tokenization, such as increased speed and reduced costs in financial transactions, while also cautioning about the risks associated with these markets. Tokenization is portrayed as a significant step in the evolution of money, offering the possibility to streamline processes by reducing the reliance on intermediaries like clearinghouses and registrars. Instead, these functions can be automated through code, potentially leading to significant cost savings and more efficient collateral use.

The IMF acknowledges the advantages of tokenized markets but warns that they can be susceptible to flash crashes and exhibit higher volatility compared to traditional markets. Automated trading, which is integral to tokenized markets, has already resulted in sudden market plunges known as flash crashes. The IMF cautions that the instant execution of trades in tokenized markets could exacerbate volatility. Additionally, the complex chains of smart contracts that underpin these markets may interact in unforeseen ways, potentially transforming localized issues into systemic shocks.

The video also addresses the risk of fragmentation, which could arise if numerous tokenized platforms develop without interoperability, thereby undermining liquidity and failing to fulfill the promise of faster, cheaper markets. Furthermore, the IMF hints at increased governmental involvement in the evolution of tokenized markets, suggesting that governments are unlikely to remain passive observers. Historical precedents, such as the Bretton Woods agreement in 1944, demonstrate governments' active roles in shaping monetary systems. As tokenization becomes more mainstream, the IMF anticipates that governments will take a more active role in its future development.

The IMF's engagement with tokenization is not new, as the organization has been examining the structure of tokenized markets and digital money for years. The release of this explainer video signifies that tokenization is now considered a mainstream policy issue. Tokenized markets have evolved into a multibillion-dollar industry, with major players like BlackRock's BUIDL fund emerging as significant entities. The IMF suggests that while tokenization offers the promise of faster, cheaper, and more programmable markets, these markets will likely be subject to stringent regulatory oversight, with governments poised to intervene as necessary.

data.title: CME Group's BrokerTec EU Market Opens Amid Trading Halt
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the CME Group announced that the BrokerTec EU market is now open for trading. However, trading in all other CME Group markets remains suspended due to a cooling system failure at the CyrusOne data center. Updates will be provided as new information becomes available.

Earlier today, trading of commodity futures at the CME, including futures for cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana, was halted due to technical issues.

data.title: Bitget has fulfilled its donation commitment: the HK$12 million donation to support Tai Po, Hong Kong has been fully disbursed.
data.description: Previously, Bitget announced a donation of HK$12 million to Hong Kong to support emergency relief efforts, assistance to affected families, and post-disaster reconstruction following the Tai Po Hung Fuk Court fire. The donation was to be handled and implemented by three reputable local charitable organizations. The HK$12 million has now been fully disbursed: HK$5 million to Yan Chai Hospital, HK$3.5 million to the Salvation Army of Hong Kong, and HK$3.5 million to Po Leung Kuk.

data.title: KuCoin obtains MiCAR license, fully expanding its compliant digital asset services in Europe.
data.description: KuCoin announced that its EU entity, KuCoin EU, has officially obtained a MiCAR license in Austria, enabling it to provide fully compliant digital asset services in all 29 EU countries. This development signifies that KuCoin has reached one of the highest levels of regulatory standards globally, bringing a safer and more transparent service experience to European users. Moving forward, KuCoin will continue to drive the robust and healthy development of the global crypto industry through its $2 billion Trust Program and numerous top-tier security certifications.

data.title: Solana Reclaims Crucial Resistance Despite First SOL ETF Outflows – 25% Rally Ahead?
data.description:
As the crypto market rebounds from the recent lows, Solana (SOL) has reclaimed a crucial level, nearing a key resistance area that could set the stage for a long-awaited price recovery rally, according to some market watchers. Related Reading: Ethereum’s End-Of-Year Rally Still At Play? Analysts Eye 50% December Jump Solana Bounces Despite ETF Outflows The crypto market has surged above the $3 trillion mark for the first time in a week, with Bitcoin, Ethereum, and most leading cryptocurrencies reclaiming crucial support levels lost during the latest market pullback. Solana joined the market rally and jumped from the recently recovered $135-$140 area to the upper zone of its local range on Wednesday afternoon. Notably, the altcoin has been trading between the $130-$145 price range over the past two weeks, briefly losing the lower boundary during last week’s correction
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/solana-crucial-resistance-sol-etf-outflows-rally-ahead/

data.title: CME Group halts futures trading as cooling system breaks down
data.description:

source: https://coinjournal.net/news/cme-group-halts-futures-trading-as-cooling-system-breaks-down/
